name: Form
import: import { Form, FormField, FormItem, FormLabel, FormControl, FormMessage } from '@/components/ui/form'
description: Form wrapper with react-hook-form integration. Use FormField for each input, FormLabel for labels, FormMessage for errors.

form.description: React Hook Form instance from useForm()

example:
<Form {...form}>
  <form onSubmit={form.handleSubmit(onSubmit)} className="space-y-4">
    <FormField
      control={form.control}
      name="email"
      render={({ field }) => (
        <FormItem>
          <FormLabel>EMAIL</FormLabel>
          <FormControl>
            <Input placeholder="user@example.com" {...field} />
          </FormControl>
          <FormMessage />
        </FormItem>
      )}
    />
    <Button type="submit">> SUBMIT</Button>
  </form>
</Form>
never: Never build forms without react-hook-form — use Form wrapper
Never use raw <label> — use FormLabel (auto-UPPERCASE in Swiss-Industrial)
Never show errors without FormMessage — it handles validation display
